translated: It was quite intense.
I thought Arisa was such a straightforward girl...
Right, Shizuku?
Yeah.
The moment Arisa and the others met
the members of Tiade...
Arisa was like, "I'm going all out,
so you'd better do the same."
She really puts those feelings into words for you.
I thought her straightforwardness, always giving it her all,
was...
really cool.
But...
Well...
hiding things like that...
The way she went about it
wasn't the best way to handle it.
So...
It's not a bad thing to have those feelings because you want to succeed.
I understand that those "damn it" feelings come from being serious about it.
I have to learn from that. I felt like it wasn't just someone else's problem.
If you direct those feelings of jealousy the wrong way,
really bad things can happen. It's difficult to handle those feelings.
You just have to find a way to deal with them inside yourself.
That "I won't lose" feeling can lead to growth
and become the energy to work hard. I want to be that kind of person too.
I have those feelings myself sometimes.
Like, "I'm so jealous of her," and things like that. Really.
Seriously.
Every single day, really.
I feel it.
But on the other hand, Shizuku...
This time, towards the girls who were seriously aiming for the center position...
She said some things she shouldn't have.
Of course, she didn't mean any harm.
I think everyone who knows Shizuku
understands that about her.
It's not that she had bad intentions.
Shizuku was just so overwhelmed at the time,
and she could only see what was right in front of her. That's why she blurted out that someone else might be better for the center.
It just came out.
I understand that feeling too.
The pressure of being the center is insane.
Between the expectations and her persona... Shizuku wasn't being her true self.
She had a character she maintained thanks to everyone, which made it even harder.
That was part of it too.
Truly.
